name: Christopher Edwards
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Infrared remote sensing • Planetary surfaces • Mars and lunar missions
bio: Christopher S. Edwards is an Associate Professor of Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University. He serves as a science team member and instrument scientist for the Emirates Mars Infrared Spectrometer on the Emirates Mars Mission “Hope,” and is a participating scientist on the Mars Science Laboratory Curiosity Rover and the OSIRIS-REx mission to Bennu. He is also a Co-Investigator on the Lunar Trailblazer mission and has contributed to multiple Mars missions, including Mars Odyssey, Mars Global Surveyor, and Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter. Dr. Edwards has been involved in planetary science research throughout his career, including during his Ph.D. at Arizona State University, as a postdoctoral fellow at the California Institute of Technology, and as a research scientist at the U.S. Geological Survey Astrogeology Science Center. His research focuses on the composition, physical properties, and processes of planetary surfaces, particularly rocky bodies such as Earth and Mars. He specializes in infrared remote sensing and has contributed to the development of instruments for space, laboratory, and field use, integrating spectroscopy, radiometry, field observations, and modeling across diverse field sites including the western United States, Hawaii, Spain, and Bhutan.
